latestPosts.title: Using Open Source Models with Claude Code: A Developer's Guide
latestPosts.description: Learn how to leverage open source AI models with Claude Code CLI for enhanced productivity, from local inference to custom agents. Perfect for developers who value privacy and customization.
latestPosts.excerpt: Modern AI development doesn't have to mean sending all your code to proprietary APIs. With Claude Code and open source models, you can enjoy the power of AI-assisted development while keeping your data local and private.…

latestPosts.title: Multi-Region Architecture: Active-Active, Active-Passive, and Consistency Trade-Offs
latestPosts.description: A practical guide to multi-region system design: active-active vs active-passive, DNS failover, RPO/RTO, data replication, conflict resolution, global databases, and when not to go multi-region.
latestPosts.excerpt: Multi-region architecture is expensive insurance. It can improve availability and latency, but it also makes data consistency, deployment, observability, and operations much harder. Before designing multi-region, ask two…

latestPosts.title: Kafka Consumer Lag: Causes, Diagnosis, and Production Fixes
latestPosts.description: A practical Kafka consumer lag playbook covering partition skew, slow processing, rebalances, max.poll settings, poison messages, autoscaling, metrics, and safe recovery strategies.
latestPosts.excerpt: Kafka consumer lag is not a root cause. It is a symptom. Lag means consumers are not keeping up with producers for one or more partitions. The fix depends on why: slow processing, too few consumers, partition skew, rebal…

latestPosts.title: System Design: Building a Feature Store for Real-Time Machine Learning
latestPosts.description: Design a practical feature store for production ML systems: offline and online features, point-in-time correctness, streaming updates, Redis/DynamoDB serving, monitoring, and training-serving skew.
latestPosts.excerpt: Production machine learning systems fail when the model sees different data in training than it sees in production. A feature store helps solve that problem. It provides a consistent way to define, compute, store, and se…

latestPosts.title: System Design: Building an Audit Log System for Compliance and Debugging
latestPosts.description: Design a production audit log system with immutable events, schema design, write paths, search, retention, tamper resistance, PII controls, partitioning, and compliance tradeoffs.
latestPosts.excerpt: Audit logs answer a simple question: who did what, to which resource, from where, and when? That question matters during security investigations, customer support, compliance audits, data recovery, and debugging. A norma…
